corrections.text: The article attributes six direct quotes to the GitHub release notes — "The Rust rewrite of GNU Coreutils," "Their accompanying FindUtils implementation," "our newly written Grep implementation," "expect some bugs in this first version," "a shim to ensure your existing DOS sort & find invocations continue to work," and "a PowerShell wrapper allowing you to use glob patterns the way you do on other OS." These quotes appear on the v2026.5.29 release/tag page, but the cited source (https://github.com/microsoft/coreutils) is the repository's main README page, which does not contain this prose. The verbatim wording could not be verified against the captured source snapshot. The underlying facts — the Rust rewrite, the findutils and grep components, the preview/bugs caveat, the DOS sort/find shim, and the PowerShell glob wrapper — are independently corroborated by the repository README and by BleepingComputer and heise.
